title: The Vaughn Kohler Podcast
author: Vaughn Kohler
description:
Most people I meet are carrying more than they let on.
They're founders, parents, pastors, executives, and leaders. They're successful in many of the ways that matter, yet beneath the surface they often feel lost. Not in a dramatic sense, but in the ordinary modern sense. Pulled in too many directions. Disconnected from what matters most. Moving quickly without always knowing where they're headed.
I know the feeling.
Each week, I share a brief reflection on faith, wisdom, relationships, meaningful work, and the lifelong process of becoming fully human. Drawing on lessons from ministry, leadership, books, suffering, beauty, and everyday life, I explore the questions that shape a meaningful life: Who am I? What do I want? What am I afraid of? What's next?
The subject changes. The mission doesn't.
Everything here is an attempt to help us become less scattered, less lonely, more rooted, and more fully alive.
If you're trying to strengthen your faith, deepen your relationships, lead with wisdom, or simply find your footing in a noisy world, you're welcome here.
Let's find our way together.
Subscribe to My Substack: https://vaughnkohler.substack.com/
Follow me on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/vaughnkohler
Visit my website: https://www.vaughnkohler.com/

title: The Poppy Day Bomb
author: The Times
description:
A bombing kills 11 people attending the Remembrance Sunday commemorations in the small town of Enniskillen, Northern Ireland. A twelfth victim would die in a coma 13 years later.


No one has ever been charged.


A lifetime later, The Times’s reporter Mario Ledwith returns to his hometown, asking who carried out these bloody murders, how the authorities failed to fully investigate, and why a silence continues to surround what happened. 

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: The Personal Finance Project
author: The Personal Finance Project
description:
The Personal Finance Project is a podcast dedicated to providing specialized advice on retirement planning, investment strategies, and financial management, particularly for employees in the oil, gas, and chemical industries. Hosted by experienced advisors from Baird Retirement Management, each episode delves into topics designed to help listeners make informed decisions about their financial futures. Whether you're approaching retirement or looking to optimize your investment portfolio, this show offers valuable insights tailored to your unique situation. Tune in to stay informed and take control of your financial well-being.
Meet Your Hosts:
•Greg Ashcroft, CFA®, CFP®, CPWA®, CIMA®, RMA®:
Greg is a Senior Vice President at Baird Retirement Management. He joined Baird in May 2015 after graduating from Texas A&M University with a Bachelor of Business Administration in Management. Greg has earned multiple professional designations, including Chartered Financial Analyst (CFA®), CERTIFIED FINANCIAL PLANNER™ (CFP®), Certified Private Wealth Advisor® (CPWA®), Certified Investment Management Analyst® (CIMA®), and Retirement Management Advisor® (RMA®). In March 2023, he graduated with a Master of Science in Personal Financial Planning.
•Scott Bartosh, CFP®:
Scott is a Director at Baird Retirement Management and holds the CERTIFIED FINANCIAL PLANNER™ certification, demonstrating his commitment to providing comprehensive financial planning services. Scott also holds a Master of Science in Personal Financial Planning, highlighting his dedication to advancing his expertise in helping clients navigate their financial futures.
Together, Greg and Scott bring a wealth of knowledge and experience to the podcast, offering listeners practical advice and strategies to navigate the complexities of retirement planning and investment management.

I share what I’m learning, trying, and noticing - from creative experiments to reflections on growth, psychology, and connection. Along the way, I pull ideas from books, conversations, and everyday moments that spark curiosity. All in the spirit of joy, learning, and being human.

title: The DefencIQ Podcast
author: Andrew Egoroff
description:
The DefencIQ Podcast

Practical, plain-English guidance for Australian SMEs navigating Defence Industry Security Program (DISP) membership, Essential Eight compliance, and the security requirements of working in the Australian Defence supply chain.

Hosted by Andrew Egoroff, co-founder of DefencIQ, each episode breaks down a single topic — DISP levels and domains, Essential Eight Maturity Level 2, security clearances, gap assessments, and the mistakes SMEs commonly make — without the jargon or the sales pitch.

Whether a Defence prime has told you that you need DISP, or you're planning ahead before it becomes a contract requirement, this podcast gives you the straight answer in the time it takes to have a coffee.

New episodes drawn from real questions we hear from Australian defence subcontractors, engineering firms, and professional services businesses entering or deepening their involvement in the Defence sector.

Learn more or book a free DISP Quick Start Assessment at defenciq.com.au

title: the Best Of BOLD perSPectives
author: Podgo
description: Boldly honest insights, inspiring life advice, wisdom, and mentorship from someone who thrives despite a devastating past. Hear jaw-dropping stories, lessons learned, occasional salty rants, with refreshing perspectives that will improve your own life and the world around you. BOLD PERSPECTIVES is a podcast for those seeking to live a better life and make the world a better place. Hosted by SAMANTHA POSTMAN, gain real and relevant knowledge, insight and a completely out-of-the-box perspective on how to shift your beliefs about relationships, parenting, work-life, and communities.Samantha is flipping the format, letting her guests interview her on the spot, giving them free access to her mind! This results in unscripted conversations full of wisdom, authenticity, and discovery! Success depends on an openness to learn, problem-solve, and make better decisions. In fact, the quality of your future life depends on how you react to new information.Tune in to BOLD PERSPECTIVES to learn and build skills of awareness to become the problem solver others look to. Samantha Postman is an avid storyteller, TEDx speaker, passionate learner, teacher, deep thinker, observer, traveler, blogger, CEO, entrepreneur, life and business coach, tax expert, farm wife of over 27 years, and a proud mother of three thriving young adults.Samantha’s life has not been simple or easy. At age of 10, she lost her beloved stepfather in a 7-car pile-up just 3 days before Christmas. She suffered severe bullying in school, death threats, and even lost her sister to a lifelong battle with drug addiction. At 35, in the prime of her life, she was struck down by a crippling disease that forced her to close her successful tax consultancy and give up all of her precious life-giving activities. It took years to heal physically, emotionally, and spiritually. With over 30 years of diverse volunteerism, she credits the charitable sector with helping her thrive despite her broken past. Not only is she a seasoned survivor of these so many personal and professional tragedies, she has leveraged her tenaciousness to become an advocate for many.Samantha is vibrant, innovative, inspiring, and resilient. She has travelled extensively, intimately connecting with individuals of diverse backgrounds, cultures, beliefs, and social and economic backgrounds, meeting people where they are to create interpersonal connections with a positive collective impact on each other’s lives. Her ability to connect with people in a genuine way makes her a beloved friend and leader. Samantha’s ability to motivate and empower others, combined with her powerful mind, compassion, testimonies, experience, curiosity, education, and vast array of acquired skills give her the unique ability to lead and inspire others.She is the Founder and CEO of SmartArrow, Inc., an ideation, innovation, and product development company specializing in cross-sector solutions. Samantha is a partner in a multi-grain farm with her husband, co-managing operations and heading up finances. Before, she owned a tax consultancy, where she worked intimately with a multitude of diverse people providing her a familiarity of a wide range of industries and organizations. It’s where she has seen and heard it all - from foolish to wise, young to old, poor to rich, divorced to married. Now, having been through the toughest life, survived, and thrived, Samantha is on a mission to help you improve your own life.BOLD PERSPECTIVES is not your usual transformational and self-development podcast.Discover life-changing solutions to success and well-being that took Samantha a lifetime to learn, test, and refine.Tune in and join Samantha as together, you will discover, explore, and grow your personal and professional self through bold perspectives. Subscribe and get ready to improve your life.Transcripts @ https://samanthapostman.com

Every episode, Georgia Barkell sits down with a woman from a significant family. Entrepreneurs, matriarchs, inheritors, the next generation. title: Living An Extraordinary Life  with Pastor Mikaela Cade
author: Mikaela Cade
description:
Life can be challenging, especially when trying to live by faith. How do you live with confidence when you have faith and the mountain doesn‘t move? How do you know what to do when you‘re wondering if God really answers prayer? How do you live with joy and peace when there is a big difference between what ”they” told you and your everyday experience? The questions seem endless. 
Each week, join Pastor Mikaela Cade, author and life strategist devoted to helping believers deepen their faith, understand their purpose, and live with passion as she discusses how to take the guesswork out of faith-filled living. Real talk about helping you bridge the gap between faith and experience. Whether you are a long-time believer, just starting your journey, or looking to strengthen your faith, this podcast is for you. #yourggrowuppastor

author: School of Pharmacy and Medical Sciences
description: Where are you now? is the podcast that follows real journeys of graduates from health, pharmacy and medical science into the careers they never expected, and the ones they always dreamed of. Each episode dives into the twists and defining moments that shaped our guests' careers. From unexpected detours and career changing decisions, to lessons learned and words of wisdom, you'll hear honest conversations that go beyond the resume. No matter where you are in your life journey, this podcast will inspire you. Because the path isn't always straight, but it can take you somewhere extraordinary.

title: The NOWCAST

The NOWCAST is a daily conversation for recovering escapists seeking clarity, presence & intentional living.
Hosted by ANDY—a human first, part monk, part artist—exploring attention, awareness, emotional regulation, modern life & the timeless practice of returning to the now.
Together, we explore the small escapes of everyday life, uncover the deeper signal beneath the noise, and find north again through reflection, practical wisdom & guided moments of stillness.
If you've ever felt distracted, overwhelmed, or disconnected from yourself, this is your daily return.

Hosted by Rachel Baptiste, Human-First Leadership strategist, Coach, and Founder of Lumen Consulting Group Inc, this show explores the hidden patterns, relational truths, and lived experiences that shape how we lead, work, and belong. These are reflective conversations for and with leaders, practitioners, and humans willing to look more honestly at what shapes lived experience and what we have learned to leave unnamed.

description: The world is shaking, changing with a power that makes the most basic things uncertain. Every day something valuable far beyond money and stuff gets taken away from us — it takes away our BALANCE. Your host, Katie Kaspari, has what it takes to restore it and seed the very core of long-term human success and happiness. As a seasoned marketer with over 20 years of experience as a CMO in the energy sector, and the founder and CEO of a successful coaching practice in the heart of London, she knows exactly what it takes to be UNSHAKEABLE and have it all – career, relationships, and happiness!

title: Smoke & Stories: Inside The Smoking Gun
author: george wilson
description: Smoke & Stories: Inside The Smoking Gun is a lifestyle and hospitality podcast inspired by the bold energy, Southern comfort, and late-night atmosphere of The Smoking Gun in San Diego. Each episode explores the personality behind the venue: the food, the drinks, the guest experience, the team culture, and the unique moments that make a great bar and restaurant memorable. The show is designed to celebrate conversation, creativity, and community while giving listeners a feel for the warm, lively spirit that defines the brand.At the center of the series is a simple idea: every great night out has a story worth sharing. Whether the conversation is about signature cocktails, crowd-favorite dishes, game-day energy, or the art of hospitality, the podcast brings those stories to life in a way that feels inviting and entertaining. Listeners can visit here to connect the experience with the official presence of The Smoking Gun and discover what makes the venue stand out.The objective of the series is to strengthen brand identity, attract new guests, and create a deeper emotional connection with the audience. title: Ideas that have shaped human history as expressed in poetry, speeches, myth and documents
author: Allan Anforth
description:
This podcast program is my personal effort to address ideas that have shaped human history as expressed in poetry, speeches, myths and documents. The programs will be posted weekly or more frequently depending on their content and the work involved


Mostly, but not always, the topics deal with what some people might describe as serious issues, which some might find depressing, unduly taxing on their concentration or offensive to their present world view. Such people are probably better to give this program a miss.

 

The program necessarily addresses existential, political, historical, philosophical, sociological and scientific issues of my choosing and no doubt, loaded with my personal biases. The purpose of is to encourage or facilitate people to think about the issues presented, for which I offer no apology for those who are offended.


Some of the literatures (poems, speeches, myths and documents) are also very long, which makes the oral presentation difficult and causes errors. It also can tax the concentration and interest of readers. For that reason, I have sometimes read only part of the relevant literature and summarised the content of the excised parts.

description: Behind the Counter is a lifestyle and culture podcast built around the experience of modern firearm ownership, curated lounge culture, and community connection. Each episode explores the people, products, and principles that shape the world around responsible ownership, personal confidence, craftsmanship, and informed decision-making.Hosted in the spirit of The Smoking Gun, the series blends practical insight with real conversations about range etiquette, gear selection, collecting, storage, training, and the social side of a destination shop. title: Joshua’s Generation Podcast
author: Joshua's Generation
description: The Joshua’s Generation Podcast with Apostle Joshua Iyemifokhae features biblical teachings, sermons, prayer messages, and discipleship content designed to strengthen your faith, deepen your relationship with Jesus Christ, and equip you for victorious Christian living. New episodes are released regularly.

title: Tender Space
author: Confidence
description:
Tender Space is a soft place for honest conversations about life, mental health, fading friendships, emotional exhaustion, healing, freedom, and the quiet struggles we carry alone.
This podcast explores the parts of being human we don’t always know how to explain — overthinking at night, feeling lost in life, outgrowing people, trying to heal while still hurting, and learning how to exist in a world that can feel both beautiful and unfair.
Through deep reflections, real conversations, and vulnerable storytelling, Tender Space reminds listeners that they are not alone in what they feel.

title: Legacy Dads with Dave and Dante
author: Legacy Dads
description: Real men with authentic faith. Legacy Dads is a community of Christian men focusing on issues such as marriage, parenting, and leadership. We equip men who are trying to balance faith, our modern lives, and leading our families to a lasting legacy.

title: Hear Me Out
author: TROY Public Radio
description:
Host Reana Wallace is bringing us together through the interests we don’t have in common.

Sharing the things we love with the people we love, this is Hear Me Out!

title: Talking Social Change
author: HoneyVic Productions.
description:
Talking Social Change is a platform for underrepresented voices and ideas that need to be heard. We have honest conversations about the social issues that matter — talks built on mutual respect, real perspective, and honesty, with a single aim: turning open dialogue into real change.
Talking Social Change is produced by HoneyVic Productions, a nonprofit that believes every community deserves a stage — using theatre, performance, and education to lift up unheard voices and turn dialogue into meaningful action.

title: Frum Our View Podcast
author: The Gzng
description: Welcome to Frum Our View podcast where we bring you sports and real life conversation from a fans perspective

title: ON TASTE. with Alexandra Wakim and Alli DelGrippo
author: Dearest Media
description: A podcast about the intersection of fashion & wine from stylist Alexandra Wakim and wine pro Alli DelGrippo.

title: Guess You Had To Be There
author: Lee Serviss
description:
Lee Serviss lives just outside Ottawa, Ontario with his wife and daughter. By day, he works as a carpenter for a local home builder. Off the job, he's spent most of his life chasing creative pursuits—from writing songs and fronting bands to performing stand-up comedy and hosting podcasts.
On Guess You Had to Be There, Lee shares stories from everyday life, explores the strange thoughts that keep him entertained, and occasionally disappears down a thought-provoking rabbit hole. It's part storytelling, part comedy, and part figuring life out as he goes.

title: Sierra Leone Community Friday Sermons (USA)
author: Sesay
description:
Assalamu alaykum, habibi and habibati,
Our aim is to stay connected through the Friday khutbahs from across Sierra Leonean communities here in the USA. With the permission of the khateebs and masjid leadership, we hope these recordings benefit all who listen.
"When the call is proclaimed for Jumu'ah prayer, come to the remembrance of Allah and leave trade. That is better for you, if you only knew." — Qur'an 62:9 insha'Allah, we will update it weekly.

title: Ayvenwood: Immersive Sleep Stories for Kids
author: Jackie Nelson
description:
Between the day and the night, there is a place called Ayvenwood... An ancient, enchanted forest that inspires wonder and belonging.  Each episode is an immersive sleep story designed for older children (9+) and the families who love them. Listeners are invited to return to Ayvenwood again and again whenever they need rest, comfort, or a quiet place to fall asleep. 


Through original storytelling rooted in gratitude, Ayvenwood offers a nightly return to the kind of joy that never really leaves us. Created and narrated by Jackie Nelson. New episodes released regularly.


Wherever you are, Ayvenwood is only a breath away.

title: Sozo Firm Insights
author: Andrew Jensen
description: Strategic growth & leadership insights from Sozo Firm for businesses, nonprofit organizations, government agencies and higher education institutions.

title: Learn English with Disney
author: Learn English With TV series
description:
Try SPEAKING exercises such as PRONUNCIATION or CONVERSATION practice and remember FOREVER all the advanced vocabulary you're learning with this lesson with SOUL!

Have you seen Soul? It's a heartwarming movie about Joe, a middle school music teacher who has always dreamed of becoming a professional jazz musician. In this lesson, we'll watch the exciting moment when his dream finally feels within reach. Along the way, you'll learn advanced expressions like heartwrenching, off the rack, and smooth sailing, helping you sound more natural in English while exploring what it really means to follow your passion.

title: Killing Time: A Horror Movie Podcast
author: Aubrey Grindstaff
description: Everything for all Scream Queens and Horror Fiends.  Hosted by Aubrey Grindstaff.

title: The Reveal Podcast with Elle Chase
author: Elle Chase
description:
What if the questions we're afraid to ask… are the ones that set us free?
I'm Elle Chase—coach, author, and sexologist—and this is The Reveal, a podcast about the intimate, often overlooked spaces where sex, identity, and emotional truth meet.
Each week, I talk with experts, thinkers, and storytellers to explore what it means to feel more whole, more seen, and more free.
No shame. No performance. Just open honest conversations about being human - because, the things we hide, often hold the most truth.
Welcome to The Reveal.

title: Now Why Would you Say That?!
author: Kandy
description: Just a Black mom, nurse, friend, veteran, late in life lesbian and educator in perimenopause living out lout!

title: On The Mark
author: West Australian Football League
description: Football broadcasting legend Mark Readings sits down with some of the biggest names across the WAFL and WAFLW competitions for candid and in-depth chats about their stories and life outside of footy.

title: Foul Play: Crime Series
author: Shane L. Waters, Wendy Cee, Gemma Hoskins
description: Welcome to Foul Play: Crime Series, the riveting true crime podcast that takes you on a deep dive into the world's most gripping cases. Each season unravels a unique case—choose one that intrigues you and dive in.

title: The Still Mind with Wendi Renay
author: Wendi Renay
description:
Wendi Renay is a creator, speaker, and mental-wellness educator who blends psychology, identity work, and grounded storytelling to help people find clarity and rebuild self-trust. She began her online presence under the brand DevOUT in 2022, supporting those healing from high-control religious systems. 
As her audience evolved, Wendi expanded into The Still Mind, a psychology-forward podcast focusing on executive function, emotional regulation, and identity reinvention. She also shares educational content on her YouTube channel at youtube.com/wendirenay.

title: Game Changers: The Future of Play
author: NOHOLD Inc.
description: Executive-level conversations about the business of games and AI — brought to you by leaders in AI and gaming.

title: Resettl - Career and Life in The Netherlands
author: Mirjam and Corine - Resettl
description:
Working in the Netherlands and wondering why nobody warned you it would be this different?
Resettl is the podcast for international professionals finding their footing in Dutch work and life. We're Mirjam and Corine, career coaches and former expats. Our backgrounds are in HR and marketing, so we know how Dutch employers really decide, and how to position you so the right people notice your value.
We share insights and real examples from our coaching practice: the Dutch job market, Dutch (work)place culture (yes, they're very direct), and building a life that genuinely fits.

title: The Velvet Parlour Book 1 - Sacred Vows, Dirty Money
author: Dr. Rome
description: Original short-form audio drama. The Sounds of Seduction, Suspense, and Storytelling. Where secrets come dressed for the occasion.  Every chapter reveals the complexities of our member billionaire's and power broker's lives full of revenge, betrayal, survival, romance, power, faith, as clarity arrives from the last direction anyone expected, narrated by "Shadow". New chapters every Wednesday and your first two chapters are free. No coins, No ads, follow at home, in the gym, while commuting, during lunch and share with friends, but do not miss a chapter. Pour a glass of your favorite hot or cold beverage, your seat awaits you in The Velvet Parlour, where the Parlour is always listening. TheVelvetParlour.VIP

title: JoinIn
author: Juniper Education
description:
JoinIn is Juniper Education’s podcast, hosted by CEO Sarah Barton, helping school and trust leaders navigate key developments in education. 
Each episode explores policies, challenges and innovations from SEND and attendance to leadership, data and AI; featuring expert insights and real-world experience. 
Designed for education professionals, JoinIn keeps you informed, connects critical issues and supports confident decision-making in a rapidly changing landscape.

title: Bedtime Dreamy Paws
author: Nna Humphrey Chikezie
description:
Welcome to Bedtime Dreamy Paws, a peaceful bedtime storytelling podcast designed to help children relax, feel safe, and drift into a restful sleep.
Each episode features a gentle, original bedtime story filled with kind animals, magical adventures, cozy forests, moonlit skies, and calming journeys created to inspire sweet dreams.
Whether it's bedtime, naptime, or a quiet moment to unwind, Bedtime Dreamy Paws is here to make every night a little more peaceful.
New bedtime stories are added regularly.

title: The Emily Boys
author: The Emily Boys
description: Emily Boys is an Emily in Paris rewatch podcast for all! First-time watchers, long-time lovers, full-time haters, but more specifically, it’s for those of us who hate that we love it but can’t stop watching, even when it insults our taste and intelligence (Which is often). Join Robby Slowik (TV Writer and Stand-Up Comic) and Lee Sampson (Works in marketing or something dumb, who cares?) as they share their complicated love for the series with their French friend Richard Mona who, until this podcast, has refused to watch even a second of the show.

title: The Jesus House Podcast
author: Jacob Pilz and Erin Campbell
description:
Bearing witness to the truth and love of Jesus ✞ | Episodes out every Thursday!
Where to find us: https://beacons.ai/thejesushousepodcast

title: Soul
author: Selkie
description:
Invisible. Immeasurable. Non-existent? 
Do people have a soul? Do things have a soul? Wat is consciousness? What is ecstasy? How can you experience unity? What happens when we die? 
Audiomakery Selkie embarks on a quest to map the realm of the soul.

title: The Strange Case of Dr. M. K. Jessup: Mysteries of the Cosmos
author: Gray Barker
description:
Dr. M. K. Jessup’s lifeless body was discovered in his station wagon on a quiet evening in 1959, leaving behind more questions than answers. A renowned astronomer and a passionate investigator of flying saucers, Jessup's death was quickly ruled a suicide, but the circumstances surrounding it spark a web of intrigue and speculation. In "Strange Case of Dr. M. K. Jessup," Gray Barker dives deep into the enigmatic life of this brilliant mind, exploring his groundbreaking theories on extraterrestrial phenomena and the mysteries of the universe.

As listeners embark on this gripping audio journey, they will uncover the details of Jessup’s controversial work and the connections he made with the world of UFO research. Barker presents a tapestry of facts interwoven with thoughtful speculation, inviting curious minds to ponder the true nature of Jessup's contributions and the shadows that accompanied his untimely demise. The audiobook delves into themes of scientific inquiry, the struggle for truth, and the thin line between genius and madness.

What drove Jessup to explore the unknown? What secrets lay hidden in the very fabric of his research? With every chapter, listeners will find themselves captivated by the mysteries of the cosmos and the man who dared to question what lies beyond our world. Join us as we unravel the threads of Jessup’s life, leaving you pondering the ultimate question: was it really suicide, or something far more sinister?

title: Tête à Tête
author: Ines Bianciotto
description:
Ce podcast est entièrement consacré à l'explication de troubles et maladies mentales, trop souvent méconnus. 
Ici, on crée une communauté bienveillante qui célèbre les profils neuro-atypiques et permet à ceux qui ont besoin d'entendre des personnes qui vivent la même chose qu'eux de le faire. 
This podcast is entirely dedicated to explaining mental disorders and illnesses that are all too often misunderstood.Here, we are building a supportive community that celebrates neuroatypical profiles and provides a space for those who need to hear from people going through the exact same experience.

title: If you’re ambitious but lazy watch
author: Jay Shetty Podcast
description:
What’s one small step you can take right now to break the cycle you’re stuck in?

Today, Jay shares a simple, science-backed method to help you overcome laziness, distraction, and burnout by focusing on consistency instead of motivation. Rather than waiting for the perfect moment or a burst of inspiration, Jay explains how lowering the bar, creating rituals, and going on a dopamine detox can help you rebuild discipline and self-trust, one small action at a time.

It’s not about working harder or chasing perfection, but about starting smaller and showing up consistently. Jay explains how to protect your focus, rewire your reward system, and break the patterns that keep you stuck. Over time, these tiny shifts compound into real transformation, helping you feel more grounded, confident, and in control.

title: PitchCon Audio: Fantasy Baseball Library
author: Pitcher List
description:
Every hour of Pitcher List's Pitch Con online fantasy baseball conference in one place, raising money for the fight against ALS.
pitcherlist.com/pitchcon

title: The Interlocutor Podcast
author: Tyrel Cameron Eskelson
description:
The Interlocutor Podcast is a series of long-form conversations about people shaped by place. Through discussions of upbringing, travel, identity, memory, history, and cultural change, guests reflect on the experiences that formed them and altered the way they see the world. Conversations feature writers, historians, travelers, journalists, and cultural voices from around the world, with a particular interest in Taiwan and East Asia. Past guests include Kevin Kelly, Emily Wu, Shelly Rigger, and Eryk Michael Smith.
Hosted by Tyrel Cameron Eskelson, author of Taiwan 22: Travels in Paradox

title: Between Two Pumps
author: Hemmings
description:
Between Two Pumps is where real car conversations happen—hosted by Terry Shea, Mike Musto, and Terry McGean. The show combines decades of market knowledge with candid debate and real marketplace intelligence. From muscle cars and European classics to modern collectibles and emerging trends, nothing is off limits.
In an environment where hype can distort perception overnight, Between Two Pumps cuts through the noise—positioning Hemmings as both a historian and a modern-day navigator of the collector-car landscape. Each episode blends firsthand experience, honest opinions, and deep industry insight to help enthusiasts understand what truly matters.
What's worth buying? What's overrated? What will stand the test of time? Terry, Mike, and Terry break it down—no scripts, no fluff.
Subscribe for new episodes and stay ahead of the collector-car market.

title: Curious Dream and Other Sketches: Witty American Humor
author: Mark Twain
description:
In "A Curious Dream," Mark Twain reveals the bizarre and humorous twists of his imagination that challenge reality and provoke thought. This collection of sketches showcases Twain's unparalleled wit as he navigates through the absurdities of life, politics, and human nature. From the outrageous escapades of a hapless senator to whimsical reflections on society, each story invites listeners to laugh and ponder in equal measure.

Twain's sharp observations cut through the mundane, uncovering the intricacies of everyday life with a satirical lens. With tales like "My Late Senatorial Secretaryship," he explores the chaotic intersection of ambition and folly, leaving us to question the absurdities of authority and power. What happens when dreams collide with the stark truths of our world?

As you dive into these sketches, prepare for a journey filled with clever wordplay, unexpected humor, and profound insights. Twain's voice resonates through time, making his observations just as relevant today as they were in his era. Discover the timeless charm of one of America’s greatest humorists and experience why his stories continue to captivate listeners. Each sketch teases your curiosity, encouraging you to reflect on the quirks of humanity while keeping you entertained. Join Twain on this delightful ride through the humorous and curious realms of his mind.

title: Blackmailer: A Dangerous Game
author: George Axelrod
description:
When sultry actress Veronica Hayes storms into Richard's office demanding fifty grand, he knows he’s stepped into a dangerous game. But nothing could prepare him for the chaos that follows. In 'Blackmailer,' George Axelrod unveils a web of deceit where love and betrayal intertwine, and every choice could lead to ruin. Richard, an unsuspecting player in this high-stakes drama, finds himself at the center of a sinister plot involving jealous lovers, ruthless gangsters, and a thirst for revenge.

As he navigates the treacherous waters of Hollywood's elite, Richard must confront not just the threats to his life but also the dark secrets that bind him to Veronica. With hidden motives lurking at every turn, listeners will be drawn into a world where everyone has something to gain—and lose. Will Richard uncover the truth behind the blackmail before it’s too late?

Join him on this thrilling journey filled with sharp dialogue and unexpected twists. With each chapter, the stakes rise higher, and the danger becomes more palpable. 'Blackmailer' isn’t just a tale of crime and mystery; it’s a gripping exploration of desire, ambition, and the lengths to which people will go to protect what they want. Prepare for an audiobook experience that hooks you from the first line and keeps you guessing until the very end.

title: Next Generation Parenting
author: Sarah Schindler
description:
Some of us always knew there was a different way to parent. We just didn't have the language for it yet.
This podcast is for the parent who is done with one-size-fits-all advice and ready to understand their family from the inside out. We talk about Human Design, holistic nutrition, nervous system regulation, generational patterns, and what it actually looks like to raise kids who know who they are — while you remember who you are in the process.
Honest conversations. Real stories. No performance.
Sarah Schindler is a Cognitive Parenting Coach, Therapeutic Nutritional Counselor, and Human Design Analyst helping families come back to themselves. She's also a homeschooling mama figuring this out right alongside you.
Each episode explores everything from understanding your child's unique design to breaking patterns that were never yours to carry — all in service of raising the next generation a little more free than the last.
Explore the work at wild-rooted.com

title: Roger Retrospective
author: Roger Horn
description: The Roger Retrospective connects the dots between history and today's headlines — because the past isn't prologue, it's a playbook. Each episode takes a pivotal moment from world history and traces its arc into the present, revealing the patterns, miscalculations, and ironies that keep repeating. Hosted by Roger Horn, a veteran emerging markets strategist with 20+ years on the front lines of global finance and a passport stamped in 50+ countries.

title: Beyond The Bicycle
author: Tyson Martin
description:
This podcast isn't about the latest bike, the fastest rider, or the biggest social media following.
It's about the people.
We explore the lives, experiences, and journeys that bicycles make possible. Every episode is a conversation about identity, resilience, creativity, adventure, community, purpose, and the moments that shape who we become.
Whether you're an everyday rider, an industry leader, a photographer, an athlete, a frame builder, an advocate, a traveler, or someone whose life has been transformed by two wheels, we'd love to hear your story.

title: Bestie Krewe
author: Bestie Krewe
description: Besties for 30 years, many stories and zero filters

title: The Direct Selling Accelerator Podcast
author: The Direct Selling Accelerator Podcast
description: The Direct Selling Accelerator podcast, hosted by Samantha Hind, is your go-to resource for accelerating your results with direct selling through online marketing, particularly on social media. From tips and strategies, to traps to watch out for, to the latest techniques to improve your results, Sam has you covered.

title: The Rangers Wire
author: therangerswire@gmail.com
description: The Rangers Wire is your home for honest, passionate Glasgow Rangers FC coverage. Match analysis, transfer updates, fan debates and more — all from dedicated Gers supporters

title: The Book of Dragons ( Enchanting Tales of Mythical Creatures )
author: E. Nesbit
description:
A dragon emerges from the pages of a magical book, setting off a whirlwind of adventures that will captivate young listeners. In 'Book of Dragons' by E. Nesbit, each tale introduces a new, whimsical dragon with its own quirks and challenges. From a dragon whose gentle purr can calm even the fussiest baby to one that devours an entire pack of tame hunting-hippopotamuses, this collection showcases the unique charm and humor that only E. Nesbit can deliver.

As children follow these enchanting stories, they’ll discover the value of courage, friendship, and imagination. Each dragon possesses distinctive traits that make them both lovable and mischievous, leading to delightful conflicts and unexpected resolutions. Listeners will find themselves rooting for the brave characters who must navigate magical realms filled with mystery and wonder.

With vivid storytelling and engaging plots, 'Book of Dragons' invites children to explore the boundaries of fantasy, sparking their curiosity and creativity. These tales are not just about dragons; they are about the journeys of bravery, the importance of kindness, and the joy of adventure. Perfect for family listening or solo exploration, this audiobook is a treasure trove of laughter and life lessons, sure to enchant listeners of all ages. Dive into the world of dragons, where every tale is a new adventure waiting to unfold!

title: Precision Rewired with Dr. Kathleen Pajer
author: CHEO Research Institute
description:
What will the future of child and youth mental health care look like?
The CHEO Research Institute's Precision Child and Youth Mental Health (PCYMH) Collaboratory is launching Precision Rewired, a new six-episode podcast series hosted by Dr. Kathleen Pajer, Medical Director of the PCYMH Collaboratory.
With a new episode released every two weeks, the series will feature leading researchers and innovators from across Canada exploring how data, artificial intelligence, genomics, and lived experience are helping reshape mental health care for children and youth.
As mental health challenges among young people continue to grow, researchers are working to move beyond one-size-fits-all approaches to care. Precision Rewired offers an accessible look at the discoveries, technologies, and collaborations that are helping make more personalized, data-informed care possible.
Throughout the series, listeners will hear from experts working at the forefront of precision mental health, tackling questions such as:
* How can genetics, environmental factors, and real-world data be integrated to improve care?
* What role can AI play in supporting earlier intervention and better outcomes?
* How can research and care become more personalized, equitable, and effective for children, youth, and families?
You can listen to Precision Rewired on Apple Podcasts, Spotify, or Youtube

title: Proudly in Conversation
author: Stonewall
description:
We bring listeners influential guests, advocates, policymakers, activists and industry leaders to share LGBTQ+ stories, insights and perspectives to remind us how far we’ve come and why these stories matter now more than ever. 
By highlighting the issues that matter in the world today, from legislation to lived experience, we spark conversation and inspire the next generation of activists.

title: PanaCME Perspectives: Clinical Conversations in Alzheimer’s Disease
author: panacmeperspectives
description:
Bridging the gap between emerging science and real-world clinical practice

The first series of the PanaCME Perspectives podcast brings together leading experts to discuss contemporary issues in Alzheimer’s disease diagnosis and care through a practical, clinician-focused lens.

Hosted by Dr John Harrison, the series features perspectives from world-renowned experts, including Jonathon Schott, Craig Ritchie, Ishtar Govia, Jill Rasmussen, Amanda Heslegrave, Dennis Chan, and Clive Ballard.

From early detection and biomarker integration to personalised care planning and equitable access, each episode delivers evidence-based insights alongside practical guidance to support everyday clinical practice, helping clinicians make more confident, informed decisions for their patients. 

Listen now and start transforming your practice with PanaCME Perspectives.

For more independent educational resources from PanaCME, visit: www.panacme.education.

title: ASI project delivery guidance
author: Neil
description: Listen for introductions to ASI guidance documents for different elements of project delivery. If you are an ASI person then you can access full guidance material on the ASI Hub. If you are not an ASI person then we hope you find these brief introductions useful.

title: The Curious Catholic
author: The Archdiocese of Kansas City in Kansas
description: We aim to educate, inspire, and accompany both lifelong Catholics as well as those searching for deeper meaning and truth. This podcast is a communications effort of the Archdiocese of Kansas City in Kansas. We have centuries of truth to explore; let's walk together.

title: Grace Missionary Baptist Stillwater Sermons
author: Grace MBC Stillwater
description: Sunday sermons and Wednesday night teachings from Grace Missionary Baptist Church in Stillwater, OK

title: That AuDHD Teacher
author: Emily Sigglekow
description: Welcome to That AuDHD Teacher, a podcast about making sense of life through the lens of a late AuDHD diagnosis. I'm Emily, an early childhood teacher and someone who spent years wondering why life always seemed a little harder than it looked for everyone else. After being diagnosed with Autism and ADHD as an adult, so many pieces of my life started to make sense. Each episode is an honest conversation about neurodivergence, mental wellbeing, teaching, and the everyday experiences that often go unseen. So lets get comfortable and make sense of this beautifully complicated brain together.

title: Zen Stories & Buddhist Teachings for Sleep by Midnight Monk
author: Midnight Monk
description:
Relaxing Zen stories and Buddhist teachings for sleep. Slow down, let go, and fall asleep tonight with Midnight Monk, a quiet space for rest, reflection, and inner peace.
Created for overthinkers, restless minds, and anyone who needs a calm place at the end of the day, each long-form episode combines gentle Zen teachings, Buddhist wisdom, and calming bedtime stories for sleep, meditation, and nighttime listening.
Experience stillness, presence, and peace through soothing narration designed to quiet your mind and ease you into rest.
An original production by Midnight Monk 🌛✨🧘

title: The Best Of Rita Rudner
author: Spotted Dog Entertainment
description: The Best of Rita Rudner is a collection of Rita Rudner's funniest stories and most memorable standup moments.

title: Accidentally Influential
author: Kate Robb and Teanna Scot
description:
Accidentally Influential is the show for EDUcreators, experts, and business owners who never set out to be "influencers"… but built influence anyway.

Hosted by Kate Robb and Teanna Scot, we've grown a 479K+ audience and landed over half a million in brand deals; now we're pulling back the curtain on how YOU can turn your authority into income.

From sponsorship strategy to creator psychology, this is your no-fluff guide to building a creator business that stacks ontop of what you're already doing. You might've become accidentally influential… but what you do with it now? That part is intentional.

title: Profit By Design
author: Eli Rubel
description:
Profit by Design is hosted by Eli Rubel, a entrepreneur who's started, grown, and sold businesses across SaaS, marketing, and design, learning the hard way each time. Now he runs Profit Labs, helping agency owners get real clarity into their finances, and is building CollectedHQ for recruiting firms. Each episode digs into the actual decisions, mistakes, and lessons behind building profitable businesses that don't depend on you.

If any of this resonates, learn more at https://www.linkedin.com/in/elirubel/

title: Sermons from Murrumburrah
author: The Anglican Church of Murrumburrah-Harden
description:
Listen to sermons preached at Murrumburrah-Harden Anglican!
If you are looking for sermons from Salt please go to: https://www.youtube.com/@SaltChurch-m5r

title: Thanks Divorce
author: Janine Bell
description:
Divorce isn't the end of the world. It's actually the beginning of a new one!

“Thanks Divorce” is about the positive transformation that can happen after divorce. Listen to be empowered and inspired and for tips on how to powerfully transition into your next chapter.

Ultimately, "Thanks Divorce" for all the lessons, the growth, the mirror to see ourselves. Truly, thanks...you, gruesome S.O.B. 

Follow us on Instagram @thanksdivorcepodcast or visit us at thanksdivorce.com
#divorcepodcast #divorcerecovery #selfhelp #selflove #healingfromdivorce #healing

title: Casebook of Sherlock Holmes Part 1
author: Sir Arthur Conan Doyle
description: And so, reader, farewell to Sherlock Holmes! These are positively the last stories. There is some disagreement as to their quality, and some claim that Conan Doyle was writing more experimentally, rather than to the conventional Sherlock Holmes formula. Not all the stories are narrated by the faithful Watson, and the stories are regarded as the darkest of the Holmes canon, with some unusual villains. Please note: Section 5, The Adventure of the Three Gables, includes language which, though normal for the time it was written, is now regarded as highly unacceptable racial slurs. Anyone who finds this too offensive may wish to omit this story. Indeed, any listener who would understandably prefer to avoid my poor attempt at American accents, may prefer not to listen to a number of these stories. Conan Doyle seemed to like American villains. … (summary by Ruth Golding) This recording was originally published on Legamus.eu and was later released on Librivox when US copyright permitted. Part 2 will be recorded when the reader is able

title: The Perro Show Podcast
author: The Perro Show
description: The Perro Show Podcast brings together real people real stories and real conversation from life experiences and current events , family , sports , and everything in-between, every episode is about keeping it authentic no scripts. no filters. just genuine conversation with friends , guests, and people who stories deserve to be heard. Whether you're here to laugh , learn something new , or simply enjoy authentic conversation, there's always a seat at the table. Welcome to The Perro Show.

title: Everything
author: Adam Ventnor
description:
Comedy that isn't.
Adam Ventnor also makes music and writes books, with everything listed at adamventnor.com.

title: Allyson's Corner
author: Allyson Julia
description:
Welcome to Allyson's Corner—the place where overthinking is basically a personality trait, life's messy middle gets a microphone, and no topic is off-limits.

I'm Allyson, and this is my little corner of the internet.Some episodes will feel like a FaceTime call with your best friend. Others might dive into dating, heartbreak, healing, writing books, chasing big dreams, navigating your twenties and thirties, anxiety, pop culture, or whatever rabbit hole my brain decides to disappear down that week.

This podcast started as a way to document a new chapter of my life, but it's become something bigger: a space for honest conversations, unfiltered thoughts, and figuring things out together. No pretending to have all the answers. No perfectly curated life. Just real conversations about becoming the person you're meant to be.

So grab your coffee, pour a glass of wine, throw this on during your commute, or listen while you're cleaning your apartment at 11 p.m.

Whatever brought you here, I'm really glad you found my corner.

title: Stepping on Toes with Danielle Fishel and Val Chmerkovskiy
author: IHeartPodcasts
description:
Stepping on Toes with Danielle Fishel and Val Chmerkovskiy is your front row seat to the world ofdance! 
 
Hosted by Season 34 Dancing with the Stars contestant (and 8th place finalist!!!), Danielle Fishel, and Len Goodman Mirrorball champion, Val Chmerkovskiy, each week they’ll break down Dancing with the Stars: The Next Pro, the new competition to find the next ballroom competitor! They’ll dive deep into the choreography, judging, and performances, while covering anything, and everything, that happens in between. 
 
Plus, you never know who will stop by to add their two cents…
 
Whether you’re a lifelong fan or new to the Fox Trot, Danielle and Val will keep you on your toes all season long.

title: Next Stop: CPA
author: AICPA & CIMA
description: Essential listening for anyone thinking about pursuing their CPA license, this is the official podcast for the CPA Exam brought to you by the AICPA. Contact us at NextStopCPA@aicpa-cima.com.

title: The Impact Lab: An NYU Public Service Podcast
author: teb63
description:
How do you solve public "unsolvable" problems? With innovative cross-disciplinary thinkers. Welcome to The Impact Lab, a podcast where two NYU public service professors offer a behind the scenes look at the wildly innovative NYU Public Service Impact Lab where they bring together graduate students, community partners, the Mayor’s Office, city agencies, community leaders, and unconventional thinkers to move the needle on policy that matters to our lives. 

Each season, we go deep into one issue. Season 1 is all about finding solutions to New York City's shortage of public bathrooms. Can a fresh look from the next generation of leaders make things better? Can we get the right people in the room to not only theorize but implement the big changes needed?

title: The Haunting of Low Fennel and Other Stories: Mystery and Adventure
author: Sax Rohmer
description:
When a mysterious artifact from ancient times surfaces in London, it unleashes a series of chilling events that intertwine the fates of unsuspecting individuals. In 'Haunting of Low Fennel and Other Stories,' Sax Rohmer crafts a thrilling journey through seven interconnected tales filled with danger, intrigue, and the supernatural. Each story introduces you to a new protagonist grappling with their own fears—be it a haunted house, a cursed relic, or a shadowy figure lurking in the night.

Rohmer expertly blends action and adventure with elements of horror and the unknown, exploring themes of obsession, fear, and the dark corners of the human psyche. As secrets unfold and mysteries deepen, listeners are drawn into a web of suspense that keeps them guessing at every turn. What dark truths lie behind the veil of normalcy? Who can be trusted when the line between friend and foe blurs?

This captivating collection showcases Rohmer's signature style, rich with vivid descriptions and gripping plots that will leave you on the edge of your seat. Join the adventure as you uncover the secrets of Low Fennel and beyond, and prepare for a listening experience that resonates long after the last story ends. Perfect for fans of action and adventure fiction, this audiobook promises an unforgettable exploration of the eerie and the extraordinary.

title: The IAMT MediaTech Forum - Visual Podcast Series
author: IAMT
description:
The definitive source for independent, trusted MediaTech information, advice and thought leadership. 
Empowering start-ups to enterprises across technology, business insights and priorities, regulation, training, and innovation.

title: Evidencia audio
author: Andrea cotes
description:
Desarrollo evidencia (hace to / must / should + infonitive).

Cover art photo provided by Microsoft Copilot on Unsplash: https://unsplash.com/@microsoftcopilot?utm_source=spreaker&utm_medium=referral

title: Global Chess Arena
author: qatarmastersopen
description: Welcome to Global Chess Arena, a podcast dedicated to the exciting world of competitive chess. Each episode explores international tournaments, remarkable players, emerging talents, historic matches, and the latest developments shaping the global chess community.  From prestigious events to local competitions, we discuss how chess continues to inspire millions of players through strategic thinking, discipline, and lifelong learning. Whether you're interested in tournament coverage, player stories, educational insights, or the future of competitive chess, this podcast offers informative

title: Desirable Alien at Home in Germany
author: Ford Madox Ford, Violet Hunt
description: A travel journal of a year the author spent in Germany. With a preface and two additional chapters by her partner, the novelist Ford Madox Ford (here using the surname Hueffer). - Summary by barbara2

title: Greatest Wonders of the World
author: Esther Singleton
description: A selection of descriptions of famous, and not so famous, natural beauty spots, as described by various travelers of the age. Travelers include William Makepeace Thackeray, Charles Dickens, Sir Richard F. Burton and Alexandre Dumas. - Summary by LynneT

title: Land of the Broads
author: Ernest R. Suffling
description: The Broads area of Norfolk and Suffolk is in the East Anglian region of England. It is the location of Britain's third largest inland waterway system and largest protected wetland area. Many examples of rare flora and fauna are found only in this unique location which has also been designated as a national park.This book: The Land of the Broads, is a practical guide to the extensive but (at one time), little-known district of the Broads of Norfolk and Suffolk.Written (and now narrated) for the use of all who take an interest in one of the Quaintest and most Old-World parts of England, either from an Archaeological, Historical, Picturesque, or Sporting point of view.So, join me if you will, on another auditory cruise around these unique waterways through expansive landscapes under wide open skies. - Summary by The Land of the Broads / TND

title: Book of the Ocean
author: Ernest Ingersoll
description: The Book of the Ocean is precisely what its title promises. It contains a rather broad overview of all topics connected to the ocean, such as its geography and the history of the exploration of the oceans. Besides the oceans themselves, the book contains several chapters on the different aspects of seafaring: building ships and seafaring, war ships, merchant ships and voyages, piracy, and yachting. - Summary by Carolin

title: America of the Fifties: Letters of Fredrika Bremer
author: Fredrika Bremer
description: When Fredrika Bremer arrived in New York from Sweden in October 1849, she was already famous throughout America for her novels and for her reformist efforts in Sweden. Thus there was no shortage of invitations, and she met with the leading lights of American culture, along with countless lesser-known people. Her main objective was to see the effect of democratic institutions on society. In her two years in America, she toured New England and met Ralph Waldo Emerson, Henry Wadsworth Longfellow, James Russell Lowell, Nathaniel Hawthorne and Washington Irving; visited Shaker and Quaker communities in Mid-Atlantic states; conversed with Senators in Washington, DC, examined conditions of slaves in the South; and toured Scandinavian frontier communities in the Midwest. She also investigated America's prisons, and everywhere noted the legal status and social situations of women. Throughout her American travels, which ended in September 1851, she reported back all that she saw in letters to her sister. She then edited those letters and published them in a 1,300 page volume; "Homes of the New World: Impressions of America". In 1924 the American-Scandinavian Foundation published a selection of the letters in this edition. (Summary by Ted Lienhart)

title: In the Land of Cave and Cliff Dwellers
author: Frederick Schwatka
description: An adventurer and explorer of no mean repute, Lieutenant Frederick Schwatka leads an expedition by mule train into the forbidding Sierra Madre mountains of Mexico to one of the richest silver mining regions in the world. He offers lively (and occasionally disparaging) descriptions of the the unfamiliar flora and fauna of this often desolate region, of the cliff and cave dwellings inhabited and abandoned, and of the social customs of the various peoples he meets. He marvels at the unmatched running prowess of the Rarámuri (Tarahumari) Indians of the Barranca del Cobre--the famous Copper Canyon of Chihuahua State. He writes always with humor that keeps the narrative light and the reader smiling. (Summary by Steven Seitel)

title: Englishwoman in the Philippines
author: Enid Dauncey
description: Enid Gambier Dauncey was a travel writer who, with her businessman husband, lived in a provincial city in the Philippines for nine months from 1904 to 1905. Her account of that stay, in the form of letters to friends, is something more than a travelogue, as she made many astute observations that would normally be missed by someone simply passing through. Her stay there occurred just a few years after the Americans replaced the Spanish as the colonial masters, and she includes anecdotes that reveal that tense relationship, as well as the naivete of the Americans called upon to administer their new 'protectorate'. Mrs. Dauncey's entertaining description of Philippine social and material life, with her comments on the culture from her foreigner's perspective, are enlivened by her natural curiosity and dry wit. (Summary by Ted Lienhart)

title: Forty Thousand Miles Over Land and Water
author: Ethel Gwendoline Vincent
description: Subtitled "The Journal of a Tour through the British Empire and America," this book is a record of the author's travels with her husband through the British Empire and America in the late 1800's. In the words of the author, "It is but a simple Journal of what we saw and did." Their travels took them across the Atlantic to the U.S. and Canada, then across the Pacific to New Zealand, Australia, then on to the Dutch East Indies, the Straits settlements, the Indian subcontinent and Egypt - Summary by knotyouraveragejo

title: The Backyard Pod
author: Brandon and Jake
description: Welcome to The Backyard Pod — where two regular Midwestern 9-to-5 guys, Brandon and Jake, crack a couple beers, kick back, and shoot the shit about everything life throws their way. From long workdays, weekend projects to hunting season, backyard bonfires, sports debates, business ideas, and chasing bigger dreams, nothing’s off the table.

title: Senior Life Network Podcast
author: Fred Ortiz
description:
The Senior Life Network Podcast is here to bring real conversations, trusted resources, and practical guidance to seniors, families, and caregivers in Brazoria County—and beyond.

Hosted by Fred Ortiz, this podcast connects you with local professionals, service providers, and real stories that help you navigate healthcare, insurance, legal decisions, and everyday life with confidence.

This is more than a podcast—it’s a place to stay informed, prepared, and supported when it matters most.

What You Can Expect:
• Conversations with local experts
• Real-life stories and guidance
• Resources you didn’t know you needed
• Clear direction for you and your family

title: Oregon Trail
author: Francis Parkman, Jr.
description: The book is a breezy, first-person account of a 2 month summer tour of the U.S. states of Nebraska, Wyoming, Colorado, and Kansas when Parkman was 23. (Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Needwood Forest
author: Francis Noel Clarke Mundy
description: Needwood Forest is a book written by Francis Noel Clarke Mundy in 1776. Mundy describes the forest's natural beauty, its flora and fauna, and the various activities that take place within its boundaries. The book also contains historical information about the forest, including its use as a royal hunting ground during the Middle Ages. Mundy's writing style is descriptive and poetic, and he captures the essence of the forest in vivid detail. - Summary by GERALD MOE

title: Rough Notes Taken During Some Rapid Journeys Across the Pampas and Among the Andes
author: Francis Bond Head
description: “Galloped on with no stopping, but merely to change horses until five o’clock in the evening—very tired indeed, but . . . saw fresh horses in the corral, and resolved to push on. At half-past seven, after having galloped a hundred and fifty-three miles, and been fourteen hours and a half on horseback got to the post—quite exhausted—I could scarcely speak . . . an hour before daylight was awakened by the Gaucho, got up, had some mate, mounted my horse, and as I galloped along felt pleased that the sun should find me at my work. . .” Later in life nicknamed “Galloping Head,” for his exploits on the Argentine pampas, Sir Frances Head Bond, went to the Argentine in 1825 as mining supervisor for the Rio Plata Mining Association, a group of English speculators whose ill-planed and financially disastrous idea it was to send Cornish miners to re-open old gold and silver mines in the former Spanish colonies. His “Rough Notes,” often written in a staccato style that is surprisingly fresh, show a gusty, resourceful adventurer— riding across the Andes on mules who sank into snow above their knees at every step, obliging the riders to balance their feet on the mules’ ears; clambering down 250 feet of notched sticks to inspect a silver mine; foraging bluntly for food for his men in a land of scarcity: “We found they had got dry peaches and live goats. We put some of the former in a pot to boil . . . and because I was very hungry, I put a pistol to (the goat’s) ear, and in a short time he was roasting on the burning embers.” Sir Bond Head later served as lieutenant-governor of Upper Canada 1835-1837. (Summary by Sue Anderson)

title: Lancashire
author: Francis Archibald Bruton
description: The county of Lancashire in the north-west of England is best known as the engine room of the nineteenth-century Industrial Revolution. Steering clear of the industrial districts, F. A. Bruton takes the reader on an engaging tour of the county's beauty spots and lesser known landscapes. Taking the view that the charm of a district is nothing without its historical associations, Bruton packs his account with historical detail and literary references to, among others, Leland, Wordsworth, Ruskin, Arnold, and Mrs. Carlyle. (Introduction by Phil Benson)

title: Domestic Manners of the Americans
author: Frances Milton Trollope
description: Next to de Alexis de Tocquville's almost contemporary Democracy in America, Frances Trollope's work may be the most famous (or at least notorious) dissection of manners and morals of the United States. The work was a sensation on both sides of the Atlantic, and particularly in America, where Trollope was reviled as representing the worst of old world prejudices the new republic (though the criticism did nothing to hurt sales). Accompanied by a son and two daughters, Trollope lived in the United States from 1827 to 1831, spending most of her time in Cincinnati, where she had hoped, when joined by her husband, to open a large department store, which was also to be a place of entertainment and culture. She was, unfortunately, almost entirely ignorant of business practices, and habitually short of money, which her husband was in no position to make up. After leaving Cincinnati she traveled briefly in the eastern states, before returning to England. There is something of a happy ending; Domestic Manners was her first book, and such a success that she turned to writing, producing in her lifetime over a hundred books, which, though they never made her very rich, were more than sufficient to keep the wolf from the door. She passed on her highly disciplined writing habits to her son Anthony (who had not accompanied her to the New World), who produced forty-seven novels as well as several other works. His views of the United States, thanks in part to its attractive young women, were, of course, more benign; indeed in The Duke's Children, the lovely New Yorker Isabel Boncassen is to become the Duchess of Omnium. It's worth remembering that Frances Trollope's introduction to America was made through what was still something of frontier territory in Ohio, and few historians would argue that refinement and high culture were outstanding features of the Jacksonian era, which she saw first hand. Though some at the time feared that Domestic Manners would lead to a new Anglo-American war, fortunately the critical disputes took place on paper. Eventually passions cooled, and some Americans even came to admit that some of her judgements were just. Mark Twain, for instance, while chiding Trollope for falling for American tall tales (see the story of the crocodile's -- or alligator's -- destruction of a family in Chapter III), also remarked that the reason Domestic Manners was so hated by patriotic Americans was because it was so true.In any case, it is offered by the reader as a contribution to the American political campaign of 2012.Introduction by Nicholas Clifford)

title: Ascent of Mount St. Elias, Alaska
author: Filippo De Filippi
description: Although Mount St. Elias had been known to native Americans for thousands of years and to Europeans since 1741 when Vitus Bering saw the peak and gave it its current name, it was not successfully climbed until 1897 by a party lead by Prince Luigi Amadeo di Savoia, Duke of the Abruzzi. This is the story of that expedition. Mount St. Elias was not climbed again for another 50 years when the advent of air support made the logistics of the climb less daunting. - Summary by Fritz

title: Beasts, Men and Gods
author: Ferdinand Ossendowski
description: “Beasts, Men and Gods” is an account of an epic journey, filled with perils and narrow escapes, in the mold of “The Lord of the Rings.” The difference is: it’s all true. Ferdinand Ossendowski was a Pole who found himself in Siberia and on the losing side during the Bolshevik Revolution. To escape being rounded up and shot, he set out with a friend to reach the Pacific, there to take ship back to Europe. During his journey he fell in with dozens of other military men who shared the same objective… but nearly every one of them perished on the way. It’s up to you to decide whether Ossendowski was threatened most by the beasts, by the men, or by the gods, or indeed, by the severe and uncompromising landscapes of Siberia, Mongolia, and China. That he survived at all seems improbable. The mystical mysteries and magics of Buddhism, “The Yellow Faith”, were woven about and through his sojourn and had no little part in his survival. Time after time he was put in the delicate position of being the bargainer between warring groups, and ultimately, only incredible luck and his friendship with the Hutuktu of Narabanchi Monastery saw him through. When published in the United States, this book caused a sensation and became a best-seller. (Summary by Mark F. Smith)

title: Around the clock in Europe: A travel sequence
author: Charles Fish Howell
description: In this travelog, the author takes us on a tour of European cities, giving snapshots at given times of the day from midday til midnight.

title: World’s Story Volume XII: The United States
author: Eva March Tappan
description: This is the twelfth volume of the 15-volume series of The World’s Story: a history of the World in story, song and art, edited by Eva March Tappan. Each book is a compilation of selections from prose literature, poetry and pictures and offers a comprehensive presentation of the world's history, art and culture, from the early times till the beginning of the 20th century. Part XII compiles stories about the early history of the United States, starting with the first explorators, the fights with the native Americans, the early settlers and culminating with the struggle for independence from the European leaders. - Summary by Sonia

title: Under The Sky In California
author: Charles Francis Saunders
description: This is a 1913 travelogue by a then-well-known botanist who wrote many books about the American Southwest and California in particular. This popular book went into three printings. “…the main concern of the author has been to draw attention to an immensity of almost unexplored mountain, desert, canon and flowery plain,… This is the real California…. Like all genuine things, it has the compelling charm of the primitive and to the lover of the unartificial it appeals with freshness and power.” ( Author's Preface and david wales)

title: Wonder Stories of Travel
author: E. E. Brown, Ernest Ingersoll, David Ker, Eliot McCormick
description: A curiously interesting collection of folkloric tales and firsthand accounts written by global travelers for 19th century periodicals; compiled and edited by Emma Elizabeth (E. E.) Brown. Authors include Eliot McCormick (the father of prolific author Theodora McCormick Du Bois), Ernest Ingersoll, E. E. Brown, David Ker and others. - Summary by Brian Fullen

title: Pilgrimage Of Etheria
author: Etheria
description: This (probably) late fourth century common era (A.D.) narrative of a Christian pilgrimage is the earliest such text which survives to us. It is an important source of information about early Christian practices. This book has an extended introduction which provides invaluable context and summaries, though some of it is a bit scholarly and dry. The text is damaged with some parts missing; missing parts will be designated in this recording by this verbal usage: “dot dot dot dot” . More information: Egeria, Etheria or Aetheria was a woman, widely regarded to be the author of a detailed account of a pilgrimage to the Holy Land. The long letter, dubbed Peregrinatio (pilgrimage) or Itinerarium Egeriae, is addressed to a circle of women at home. Historical details it contains set the journey in the early 380s, making it the earliest of the kind…. It is the earliest extant graphic account of a Christian pilgrimage…. The text is a narrative apparently written at the end of Egeria's journey from notes she took en route, and addressed to her 'dear ladies': the women of her spiritual community back home. In the first extant part of the text, she describes the journey from her approach to Mount Sinai until her stop in Constantinople…. The second portion of the text is a detailed account of the liturgical services and observances of the church calendar in Jerusalem (most likely, under Cyril). The liturgical year was in its incipient stages at the time of her visit. This is invaluable because the development of liturgical worship (e.g. Lent, Palm or Passion Sunday) reached universal practice in the 4th century. Egeria provides a first-hand account of practices and implementation of liturgical seasons as they existed at the time of her visit. This snapshot is before universal acceptance of a December 25 celebration of the nativity of Jesus; this is very early and very helpful in cataloging the development of annual liturgical worship. Sections 8 and 12 of the textual introduction are omitted in this recording as they are long lists. The book's extensive footnotes are not recorded as is not the extensive concluding index ( David Wales and Wikipedia)

title: The Sporting Club Ireland Podcast
author: Mark Brandon
description:
The Sporting Club Ireland Podcast brings together the worlds of business and sport.
Every Monday, Hugh Cahill, Mark Brandon and Michael Meehan break down the biggest stories, talking points and moments from the week in sport. Every Friday, they sit down with sporting legends, elite athletes, coaches, CEOs, founders and senior business leaders to uncover the mindset, leadership, resilience and defining moments behind success.
Expect conversations covering rugby, football, GAA, boxing, horse racing, golf, Formula 1, the NFL, entrepreneurship, culture, high performance and the business of sport

title: Adventures of a Woman Hobo
author: Ethel Grace Lynn
description: Ethel and Dan Lynn wed in San Francisco in the immediate aftermath of the 1906 earthquake. The young couple soon moved to Chicago where she established a medical practice while he sought backing for a business based upon his invention. Within two years the move had proved disastrous. The Panic of 1907 dashed Dan's entrepreneurial dream and made it impossible for him to find steady work. Far worse, Ethel was diagnosed with tuberculosis and told she should return to California to improve her health. Their finances drained, they set out on a tandem bicycle that Dan retrofitted for the trek. This is Ethel's journal of their 1908 tramp across the western United States. NOTE: There are issues of race in the telling of "The Adventures Of A Woman Hobo", particularly the use of an anti-black, derogatory term. It is LibriVox's policy to record texts as written. - Summary by Brian Fullen

title: Girl's Ride in Iceland
author: Ethel Brilliana Tweedie
description: Join Ethel and her four traveling companions as they visit Iceland in 1887. Rough seas, cold temperatures, and difficult riding do not deter them. They visit small towns, the large capital, and the famous Geyser. Interspersed in the travelogue are Ethel’s observations, from her notably British and privileged viewpoint, of the people of Iceland and the beauty of its landscape. (Curious listeners may want to read the author’s extensive table of monetary conversions on page 72 of the book.) (Summary by Jennifer Fournier) Jennifer Fournier, the narrator, would like to thank Mardah Cohen Weinfield for her assistance with the pronunciation of the Latin plant names and Margrét Maríudóttir Olsen for her assistance with the Icelandic sentences. Any mistakes are Jennifer’s alone.

title: Travels in New Zealand with contributions to the geography
author: Ernst Dieffenbach
description: “Let the reader imagine a deep lake of a blue colour, surrounded by verdant hills; in the lake several islets, some showing the bare rock, others covered with shrubs, while on all of them steam issued from a hundred openings between the green foliage without impairing its freshness: on the opposite side a flight of broad steps of the colour of white marble with a rosy tint, and a cascade of boiling water falling over them into the lake!” Such is Ernest Dieffenbach’s description of his first glance of the White Terraces in Lake Rotomahana, see cover image. Johann Karl Ernst Dieffenbach (aka Ernest) traveled to New Zealand between 1839 and 1841 employed by the New Zealand Company as naturalist. He traveled in the Marlborough Sounds at the top of the South Island and extensively throughout the North Island at an early time in European settlement. In Volume I of “Travels in New Zealand” he describes his travels, integrating his observations of the natural world with the progress of colonisation, and a humane account of the Māori people that he met and their culture, settlements and inter-tribal politics. He made an important contribution to the early knowledge of the New Zealand flora and fauna, with his collections eventually being lodged in the British Museum and the Royal Botanic Gardens, Kew. Note: Māori words have been pronounced as spelled in the text, which is occasionally different to modern spelling and pronunciation. (summary by Gail Timmerman-Vaughan)

title: Wild Life on the Rockies
author: Enos A. Mills
description: "This book contains the record of a few of the many happy days and novel experiences which I have had in the wilds. For more than twenty years it has been my good fortune to live most of the time with nature, on the mountains of the West. I have made scores of long exploring rambles over the mountains in every season of the year, a nature-lover charmed with the birds and the trees. On my later excursions I have gone alone and without firearms. During three succeeding winters, in which I was a Government Experiment Officer and called the "State Snow Observer," I scaled many of the higher peaks of the Rockies and made many studies on the upper slopes of these mountains." (Summary from the Preface of Wild Life on the Rockies)

title: White Heart of Mojave
author: Edna Brush Perkins
description: "The White Heart of the Mojave" recounts a 1920's adventure "in the wind and sun and big spaces" of Death Valley by two independent minded women, Edna Brush Perkins and Charlotte Hannahs Jordan. Both women were early feminists, Edna as chairwoman of the greater Cleveland Woman's Suffrage Party (1916-18). At the end of the Great War, the two friends wanted nothing more than to escape "to the solitariness of some wild and lonely place far from city halls, smokestacks, national organizations, and streets of little houses all alike." Their vacation started as a long motor drive through the backwoods of California (Charlotte's husband, Ned, owned the Jordan Motor Car Company). It ended with a month long trek through Death Valley in an old milk wagon drawn by a horse and a mule. Edna's descriptions of the desert are superb and from the heart--the dunes "were very beautiful, with knife-edged tops ridged in pure, clean lines from which fringes of fine sand blew up like the wind tossed manes of white horses." This is a great listen for anyone who likes first-hand accounts of adventure in the Great Outdoors. (Summary by Sue Anderson)

title: In the North Woods of Maine
author: Elmer Erwin Thomas
description: Two fifteen-year-old boys---the younger of whom may have been fourteen---decide to hunt and trap away from home in the north woods of Maine. A true recounting of their adventures can be found here, though the years that passed before the tale was written down may have added a slight bit of exaggeration. Then again, they set out in the winter of 1875 and all that's written could very well be the complete and honest truth! The listener may wish to listen first to Section 18 of this recording---Notes on Maine Animals---as familiarity with these will enhance an understanding of the memoir.

title: Civilization and Climate
author: Ellsworth Huntington
