No fluff, no theory for theory’s sake. Just real conversations that move you from where you’re stuck to where you’re called.
If you’re ready to stop striving and start becoming a Transformational Leader, this is your podcast.

title: Tales Grotesque and Curious: Eccentric Parables and Dark Studies
author: Ryūnosuke Akutagawa
description:
When a demon offers a man a peculiar deal involving tobacco and temptation, the stakes spiral into a surreal exploration of morality and desire. In "Tales Grotesque and Curious," Ryūnosuke Akutagawa invites listeners into an extraordinary collection of eleven stories that blur the lines between reality and the uncanny. Each tale presents a unique lens on the human condition, where the bizarre collides with the profound.

Discover the unsettling world of "Lice," where the grotesque becomes a mirror reflecting our deepest anxieties, or step into the haunting narrative of "Rashomon," which challenges the nature of truth itself. Akutagawa doesn’t just tell stories; he crafts intricate character studies that reveal the complexities of the human psyche.

With themes of morality, identity, and the strange twists of fate, each tale holds a captivating mystery that keeps you guessing. Whether it’s the enigmatic "Wine Worm" or the poignant insights of "The Handkerchief," listeners will find themselves drawn into a web of curiosity and contemplation. This audiobook is an invitation to explore the eccentric and thought-provoking mind of one of Japan's literary giants. Join Akutagawa on a journey that promises to challenge your perceptions and ignite your imagination.

title: Sweet but Serious
author: Angel Ardito
description: Sweet but Serious is a podcast for anyone navigating or thinking about navigating a vegan lifestyle and the wild world of advocacy. Hosted by Angel Ardito, this show moves at the speed of real life. Some days are filled with with compassionate advice on how to handle the holidays. Other days, the filter is completely off, and we’re venting about the heavy stuff. Tune in every week to see which side of the coin we’re flipping... because living means feeling it all.

Embark on a highly effective and deeply impactful journey that will take you in quantum leaps toward the life you dream of living. Here, you will discover three powerful, scientifically proven, forces that will change your life and outcomes, and give a game-changing inside track to a faster, easier, and far better way of living and leading. 
 
If you are a leader, entrepreneur, seeker, or other progressive thinker dedicated to positive change in the world, the workplace, and in life; if you are actively looking for ways to make a real and lasting difference in your own life and in the life and experience of others, this podcast is for YOU.

title: Yoga by Nature Podcast
author: Morven Hamilton
description:
Can't make it to a daily yoga class? You can  practice these yoga and meditation audio classes at home or on the move.
Classes vary from 10 mins to 90 mins  long, and you can choose a Hatha yoga class, restorative, guided relaxation, yoga nidra or mindfulness meditation. L1 classes are suitable for beginners. 
You'll also find yoga for cancer classes which are part of Morven's Healing the Whole Person/ yogaThrive programme for cancer survivors. 
These classes are mostly recorded live in group sessions and I am teaching what I see in the room, so you have the benefit of being part of the group process and can refine your yoga with the group. 
Yoga can help with stress, insomnia, depression, back pain, cancer recovery, weight loss and much more.

title: Hellflower: Exile on Venus
author: George O. Smith
description:
Farradyne’s life shattered the moment his ship crashed into the Bog on Venus, leaving him as the lone survivor among 32 souls lost to the depths of rotting fungus. Once a revered Space Master, he now grapples with the stigma of his survival, haunting him like a ghost. His only solace lies in the desperate need to escape the decaying landscape of his exile.

As he navigates the treacherous world of Venus, Farradyne uncovers a dark path back to the stars—a path that forces him to confront the vilest depths of human nature. To reclaim his place among the stars, he must embrace a life of deception and manipulation, risking everything he holds dear. Each choice he makes pulls him deeper into a web of intrigue and danger, where betrayal lurks in every shadow.

Hellflower takes you on a gripping journey through the complexities of survival and redemption. With every twist and turn, the stakes escalate, pushing Farradyne to the brink as he confronts what it truly means to be human in a world where life is cheap, and trust is a luxury. Will he rise from the ashes of his shattered past, or will he succumb to the darkness that threatens to consume him? Experience the tension, the choices, and the haunting beauty of a life lived on the edge in this captivating science fiction tale.

title: The Wholly Well Blueprint

You're succeeding by every external measure — and quietly running on empty.

Michelle Ford, MPH, is a public health professional and founder of Navigating YOUR World Academy, and she's here to tell you the problem isn't your work ethic. You've been omitted from your own care plan. 

Through her signature Wholly Well Program — built on the National Wellness Institute's validated 6 Dimensions of Wellness — Michelle shows high-performing women how to stop compartmentalizing their health, identify exactly where their energy is leaking, and build the Boundary Architecture that puts them back in the driver's seat.

This series is for the woman managing everything and investing in nothing for herself.

Each episode is an intentional conversation about identity, emotional maturity, spirituality, embodiment, wealth, and the inner shifts required to move differently at your next level.Through solo episodes and intimate interviews with creators, leaders, and cultural voices, The Status Talk dives into the real work behind elevation — the standards you hold, the boundaries you set, the discipline behind ease, and the alignment that sustains success.This isn’t motivation. This is embodiment.If you’re in a season of becoming — shedding old versions, raising your standards, and stepping into a more regulated, confident, and self-led life — The Status Talk is where you belong.✨ New episodes weeklyBecause your Next Level requires a different STATUS.

AI is changing how rental businesses run, and most of the conversation about it is still stuck on which tool to use. Net Effective: The Rental AI Show goes somewhere else.

Whether you're just passing through or staying for a long holiday, we hope you enjoy your trip to the Guess Room.  Tell your friends and family and join us again next week for those digs you can't find anywhere else!  Support us on Patreon at https://www.patreon.com/theguessroompodcast and check out our merch at https://the-guess-room-podcast.myshopify.com/

title: My Autism Life with Asperger Syndrome
author: Emily
description:
The day to day problematic interactions by someone with Asperger Syndrome.
I will give accounts of when I have been part of conversations/situations where I have been overly literal that have led to misunderstandings. I will describe what happens when I try to say something in groups of people. Also the complexities that come when trying to work out what to say in any situation. 
After listening to this podcast I would like to think you will learn that people who have Asperger Syndrome/(Level 1) Autistic Spectrum Disorder are not really able to mask their communication/social difficulties.

title: God Still Cares Podcast
author: Breunna Brad
description: Welcome to my podcast where faith meets everyday life. Join me as I explore God's Word, the teachings of Jesus Christ, and the work of the Holy Spirit while having honest conversations about navigating life's challenges. As a mom, seminary student, and hairstylist, I share a unique perspective on growing in faith, pursuing purpose, and overcoming the feeling of being stuck. Together, we'll discover how a Christ centered life can lead to true transformation, spiritual growth, and practical wisdom for everyday living. Whether you are deepening your walk with God or just beginning your journey, you'll find biblical encouragement, real life insights, and hope for wherever you are.

description:
We’re excited to announce our podcast channel – Two Herb Nerds. Hosted by naturopaths and herbalists Kristin Gilmour and Julianne Grant, this podcast is born from our shared love of herbal medicine and the incredible plants we have the privilege of working with every day. Through thoughtful conversations, we explore the herbs that inspire us, draw from the traditional wisdom of herbal medicine, unpack research, discuss practical prescribing strategies, and chat with guests who share our passion for botanical medicine. Whether we're diving deep into a single herb or exploring broader clinical themes, our aim is to foster curiosity, inspire confidence, and support herbal medicine practitioners in their clinical practice.

author: Yuki Amemiya
description: When Hiroto Sakuma unexpectedly dies in modern Japan, he awakens in a vibrant fantasy realm, tasked by a goddess to unlock a forbidden class that could alter the fabric of reality itself. Teaming up with the fierce and resourceful Yuna Arisugawa, Hiroto must navigate the perilous challenges of a legendary race that traverses realms, all while foiling the sinister ambitions of Naoya Kirisame, whose twisted plot threatens the world. As alliances form and the countdown begins, Hiroto faces a race against time that will decide the fate of both his new home and the life he left behind.

title: My Health My Life
author: chibo
description: My Health My LifeMy Health My Life is a podcast dedicated to helping you live a healthier, happier, and more fulfilling life. Each episode explores practical ways to improve your physical, mental, and emotional well-being through simple, evidence-informed lifestyle choices.From healthy eating and regular exercise to stress management, quality sleep, preventive care, and positive habits, we provide clear, easy-to-understand insights that fit into everyday life. Whether you're just beginning your wellness journey or looking for fresh motivation, this podcast offers helpful tips and encouragement to support your goals.Join us as we explore the connection between health and quality of life, share strategies for lasting wellness, and inspire you to make small changes that can lead to meaningful results. Because when you take care of your health, you invest in your future—one healthy choice at a time.

BariDiaries™ — Powered by Healing. Fueled by Community.
Stories that remind you who you are — and who you’re becoming.
BariDiaries™ is where lived experience becomes shared wisdom. Hosted by CeCe Chancellor, each episode invites honest conversation, meaningful connection, and the kind of encouragement that carries you forward — long after the episode ends.
Real lives. Shared wisdom. Collective strength.
This is a space where community becomes the foundation for healing and growth.
Real voices. Shared strength. A global community of support.
Powered by Bariatric Community Nonprofit.

title: Blue Notes
author: Poppyland Community Radio
description:
Blue Notes celebrates the world of blues and blues‑inspired music, from classic pioneers to modern artists keeping the genre alive. Expect soulful vocals, expressive guitar lines and stories rooted in musical heritage.

Broadcast on Poppyland Radio, a community station at the heart of North Norfolk, Blue Notes is part of a wider mix of specialist programming that champions passion, authenticity and shared listening—reflecting the unique character of the Poppyland coast and countryside.

title: Six Feet of History – Dark History in 5 Minutes
author: Dark Whisper Studios
description:
History has a body count.
Six Feet of History digs up the disturbing events, cursed places, and dark origins behind the things you thought you knew and tells the whole story in five minutes flat.
From the factory women who glowed in the dark to towns that have been burning for sixty years, every episode is a short, gripping, true story you won't forget. No filler. No fluff. Just the dark history they buried — unearthed daily.
A Dark Whisper Studios production.
New episodes every day.
Follow now and start digging.

title: Tanjim's Podcast
author: Tanjim
description: Tanjim's Podcast is a top-ranked, no-compromise centrist politics podcast hosted by Joseph Steagall. Operating under the banner "No Left. No Right. Forward," the show cuts through partisan echo chambers to address issues like foreign policy, elections, and cultural breakdowns.

title: Emerging
author: iHeartPodcasts
description: The superpowers of tomorrow have more in common than you think. Bloomberg’s Menaka Doshi and Haslinda Amin dive deep on the challenges and opportunities facing some of the world’s most exciting, rising economies. How does India gain an edge in AI when the technology is dominated by the US and China? Is Singapore really the best model for rapid economic development? And just how important is soft power in a country’s rise? Each episode, Menaka and Haslinda sit down with leaders and experts to talk about the new economies shaping the global future. Published monthly, this series runs alongside Bloomberg’s New Economy Forum, taking place in Delhi, India for the first time this October.

title: Lance
author: UltimoUomo
description:
Lance Armstrong ha vinto sette Tour de France consecutivi dal 1999 al 2005, è stato un'icona dello sport statunitense, un simbolo della lotta al cancro. Nel 2013, però, va da Oprah Winfrey per confessare l’uso di doping durante tutta la sua carriera, e oggi i suoi successi sono un buco nero nella storia del ciclismo. Cosa ci insegna la sua storia? Cosa dobbiamo pensare della sua carriera? Cosa è rimasto della sua eredità al ciclismo?

Umberto Preite Martinez vi porta nella storia di Lance Armstrong, analizzando le vicende giudiziarie che lo hanno portato a confessarsi.

L'illustrazione della copertina è di Antonio Pronostico.

Le musiche sono di Valerio Delphi.

title: Emergency Room: The Covid Diaries
author: Guy Maddison & Matthew Hall
description: Emergency Room: The Covid Diaries, is a true-life look behind the headlines first-hand account of what it was like behind the scenes at a major American hospital as the Covid-19 pandemic swept through the United States as seen by nurses and doctors who dealt with the unprecedented crisis every day.Told through the eyes of Guy Maddison, a Registered Nurse at a level one trauma center in Seattle, Washington, Emergency Room tracks the on-the-ground medical response as the virus arrived on US shores. We learn about the massive operations required to protect healthcare workers and the public from the disease, the personal fears and anxieties of patients and staff, and what went right and what went horribly wrong.Anchored by journalist Matthew Hall, who steers us through the often harrowing and sometimes humorous journey asking the questions medical and epidemiology newbies want to know and help explain why this story remains as relevant today as April 2020.

title: The Black Alpha's podcast
author: The Black Alpha
description:
Hi, my name is Ebenezer Alpha Ansah, some call me The Black Alpha. I will be your host on The Black Alpha Podcast, your daily breakdown of the biggest internet culture, trending news, and viral stories dominating your feed. Inspired by rapid-fire internet commentary, we cut through the noise to bring you unfiltered entertainment news, creator drama updates, and deep dives into why the internet is losing its mind today. Hit subscribe to stay caught up on the daily culture shift, no filler, just the facts.


Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: MissDiagnosed
author: Miss Diagnosed
description: Miss Diagnosed is a women's health and wellness brand dedicated to empowering individuals through education, awareness, and trusted health resources. The platform aims to bridge the gap between patients and reliable healthcare information by addressing topics often overlooked or misunderstood in women's health. With a focus on awareness, advocacy, and evidence-based content, Miss Diagnosed strives to help women better understand their bodies, recognize symptoms, and make informed healthcare decisions.

title: Learn Spanish
author: Roy Coughlan
description:
Learn Spanish Podcast — Real Conversations for Real Spanish Learners

Learn Spanish Podcast is a language-learning show where a real beginner learns Spanish in real time, alongside an experienced teacher. Hosted by Roy Coughlan (the student) and Ania (the teacher and co-host, also known from Learn Polish Podcast), each episode breaks down practical Spanish vocabulary, grammar, and pronunciation through natural conversation rather than scripted lessons.

Unlike traditional language courses, this podcast follows an authentic learning journey: mistakes, corrections, and progress happen live on air, making it ideal for beginner and intermediate Spanish learners who want to hear how real language acquisition actually works.

Episodes cover everyday vocabulary, common phrases, grammar explanations, cultural context, and conversational practice for anyone learning Spanish as a second language. Perfect for commuters, travelers, expats, and self-taught learners who want an engaging, low-pressure way to build Spanish listening comprehension and speaking confidence episode by episode.

New episodes regularly. Learn Spanish the natural way, one real conversation at a time.

title: Sisterly Chaos
author: Brooklynn Probert and Kallin Freestone
description:
Sisterly Chaos is a weekly hangout with two sisters who grew up sharing everything, except a filter.
Every week, Brooklynn and Kallin will dive into life, relationships, embarrassing moments, and whatever random tangent they get stuck on. If you want a podcast that feels like sitting on the couch with your favorite people, come and join the chaos!

Forward", the show delivers militant centrism—calling out hypocrisy on both sides and exploring pragmatic, zero-BS solutions for issues like border security, foreign policy, and elections.

description:
A horror podcast with a twist! 
Join hosts Austin and Ephie on Nightmares & Nightcaps, the podcast where horror meets happy hour. Each episode, we sit down to discuss the horror cinema and television that we can't stop thinking about, pairing them with a custom cocktail to fit the mood. 
Grab your shaker, turn down the lights, and sit back for a chillingly good time.

Do you have the courage to be happy? 
Here, we believe our health truly is our happiness. We explore holistic health, peeling back layers of conventional wisdom to reveal a more comprehensive understanding of well-being. Hosted by Kerri Rachelle, Doctor of Integrative and Functional Medicine, Registered Dietitian Nutritionist, and Psychic Medical Intuitive, each episode bridges the gap between rigorous medical science and mystical intuition. From exploring the complexities of the human body to decoding the whispers of our spirit, this podcast invites you to unlock the secrets of true wellness and discover authentic joy. Join us on a transformative journey that integrates the best of scientific insight with the power of intuitive wisdom.
kerrirachelle.com/podcast

title: ShipTalk - SRE, DevOps, Platform Engineering, Software Delivery
author: By Harness
description:
ShipTalk is the podcast series on the ins, outs, ups, and downs of software delivery. This series dives into the vast ocean Software Delivery, bringing aboard industry tech leaders, seasoned engineers, and insightful customers to navigate through the currents of the ever-evolving software landscape. Each session explores the real-world challenges and victories encountered by today’s tech innovators.

Whether you’re an Engineering Manager, Software Engineer, or an enthusiast in Software delivery is your interest, you’ll gain invaluable insights, and equip yourself with the knowledge to sail through the complex waters of software delivery.

Our seasoned guests are here to share their stories, shining a light on the do's, don’ts, and the “I wish I knew” of the tech world. If you would like to be a guest on ShipTalk, send an email to podcast@shiptalk.io
Be sure to check out our sponsor's website - Harness.io

title: Boring History for Sleep
author: boringhistory
description:
Boring History for Sleep is a narrative podcast designed to help you drift into sleep through slow, calming, and immersive stories from the past

Each story unfolds across multiple episodes, creating a gradual journey through ancient civilizations, forgotten worlds, and fascinating eras of history. From Ancient Egypt to Rome, from Greece to the Middle Ages, from the Vikings to the world of the samurai, each series gently explores distant places, everyday lives, quiet details, and the atmosphere of another time.

This is not a fast-paced history lesson or a dramatic documentary. It is a soft, atmospheric listening experience created to relax your mind, slow down your thoughts, and turn history into a peaceful nighttime ritual.

Perfect for listeners looking for sleep podcasts, bedtime stories for adults, relaxing history, historical storytelling, sleep stories, slow listening, calming narration, and peaceful audio to enjoy at low volume before falling asleep

Close your eyes, follow the voice, and continue the journey one episode at a time

Boring History for Sleep — history told softly, to guide you through the night.

title: People v. Podcast: This Week in Illinois Criminal Caselaw
author: David R. Drwencke
description:
Every week, the Illinois Supreme Court and Illinois Appellate Court hand down criminal decisions that change how cases are defended, charged, and tried across the state. People v. Podcast breaks down each new published criminal decision in plain English — what happened, what the court held, and why it matters — in about three minutes per case.

Hosted by David Drwencke, Chicago criminal defense attorney, founder of DRD Law, LLC, and author on Illinois criminal defense. Built for Illinois defense attorneys, prosecutors, judges, law students, and anyone who wants to stay current on Illinois criminal law without reading every slip opinion.

New episodes weekly. This podcast is for informational purposes only — it is not legal advice.

author: Ali Christos
description:
Mentoring Myself with Ali Christos is a reflective mentorship podcast for young adults navigating purpose, confidence, relationships, money, emotions, discipline, happiness, stability, and success in an uncertain world.
This is not motivational hype. It is honest guidance, practical wisdom, and real-life lessons for building a life by design, one rooted in peace, purpose, stability, community, fulfillment, and self-respect.
These are the lessons I wish I had when I was in your shoes.

A collection of ghost stories told by real people with real encounters.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: The Tabletop
author: Vanta
description:
CISOs spend careers preparing for incidents they hope never happen. Here's what it looks like when one does.
Each episode of The Tabletop puts a security leader into a high-stakes, tabletop exercise—complete with evolving injects, tough tradeoffs, and no idea whether the incident they're navigating is ripped from the headlines or completely fictional. At the end, they have to guess.
Fast-paced and hosted by Vanta's Khush Kashyap, The Tabletop is a rare, front-row seat to how the best security minds in the world actually think, prioritize, and make critical decisions under pressure.
It’s part game, part masterclass—designed to entertain, educate, and showcase the real-world complexity of modern security leadership.

title: WW1: Unusual Tales from The Great War.
author: Andy Stuart
description: Ordinary people, extraordinary times. The war was not just the Western Front, Gallipoli, mud, blood and barbed wire. It was, as described on the tin; a World War. Japan, The White, Black, Red and Baltic Sea's. Siberia, Africa, China, Chile, Siam and Samoa. People, places and events you'll never have heard of. A new approach to what we thought we knew about. The fighting, politics, disasters, intrigues and social history of the time.

title: The Power Of The Inner Man
author: Martin Kingsley
description: This introductory episode explores the concept of the 'inner man,' emphasizing the untapped power within each individual. It sets the stage for a journey into self-discovery and personal growth, offering insights into harnessing inner strength for transformative change.

title: Forensic Video Law Podcast
author: Forensic Video Law
description:
Welcome to the Forensic Video Law Podcast, where we take you inside the world of forensic video and personal injury advocacy. Each episode explores how mediation videos and Day in the Life video help attorneys show the real-life impact of injuries, increase case value, and bring justice to clients.
Hear from attorneys, legal experts, and the team at Forensic Video Law as they share stories, strategies, and insights that make a difference in personal injury litigation.

title: (SBU) Struggling Brothers United
author: Struggling Men United
description:
Struggling Brothers United


Real Men. Real Struggles. Real Hope.


Life isn't easy, and none of us were meant to carry its burdens alone.

Struggling Brothers United is a podcast and brotherhood for men who want honest conversations, real encouragement, and lasting hope. Whether you're facing personal struggles, relationship challenges, loss, addiction, anxiety, failure, or simply trying to become a better man, this is a place where you don't have to pretend you have it all together.


Each episode explores real-life challenges through authentic conversations, practical wisdom, and biblical truth, reminding us that strength isn't found in pretending we're okay—it's found in faith, hope, and brotherhood.


This isn't about being perfect.


It's about taking the next step.


It's about growing stronger together.


It's about discovering that no matter what you're facing, you never have to walk alone.


So whether you're listening on your morning commute, working late, or searching for hope in a difficult season, welcome.


You're among brothers.


Real Men. Real Struggles. Real Hope.


Struggling Brothers United.

title: The Clock End Taproom

description:
Welcome to The Clock End Taproom, the Arsenal podcast where three mates get together to talk all things Arsenal over a virtual pint.
From match reactions and tactical debates to transfer rumours, club news and memories from years gone by, no topic is off limits. Whether Arsenal have just won 5-0 or dropped points in the most frustrating way imaginable, you'll find honest opinions, passionate discussion and plenty of laughs along the way.
We're not journalists, insiders or tactical gurus. Just Arsenal supporters who love talking about the club we can't stop thinking about.
So pull up a chair

The RV Show is weekly radio show of great things to see & do whilst traveling Australia in your RV, Caravan or 4WD, along with with some RV News, Tips and Info. The RV show is targeted to RVers, Grey Nomads,  Families and 4-wheel drive enthusiasts looking to travel Australia.

Hosted by Scott Gibbons OAM. Due to copyright limitations, music from the original radio broadcast is removed from the audio in this podcast.
